title: Spacecraft Touches the Sun's Atmosphere
story: A daring spacecraft named Parker Solar Probe made history on April 28, 2021. It became the first human-made object to actually touch the Sun! The probe crossed the Alfvén critical boundary, entering the Sun's outer atmosphere. This incredible journey helps scientists learn more about our closest star and how it affects Earth. The Parker Solar Probe braved extreme heat and radiation to gather valuable data about the Sun's mysteries.
activity_question: What is the name of the spacecraft that touched the Sun's atmosphere?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for scientists to study the Sun?

Duncan's spooky stories, like 'I Know What You Did Last Summer', kept readers on the edge of their seats. Her talent for suspense made her books favorites among young readers for decades.
activity_question: What famous book did Lois Duncan write?
activity_choices: I Know What You Did Last Summer
activity_answer: I Know What You Did Last Summer
follow_up_question: Have you ever read a suspenseful book that kept you guessing until the end?

title: A Star is Born in Sweden
story: Lights, camera, action! On April 28, 1941, a future star entered the world. Ann-Margret Olsson was born in Valsjöbyn, Sweden. She would grow up to dazzle audiences as both an actress and singer. Ann-Margret's talent shone in movies like Bye Bye Birdie, Viva Las Vegas, Tommy, and Grumpy Old Men.
activity_question: In which country was Ann-Margret born?
bonus_fact: Ann-Margret became a Swedish-American star, finding success in both acting and singing.

title: Liz Edgar: Welsh Showjumping Star
story: Hooves thundered and crowds cheered as Liz Edgar soared over obstacles. Born in Cardiff, Wales on April 28, 1943, she became a legendary showjumper. Edgar clinched the Queen Elizabeth Cup five times and galloped to victory in the prestigious Aachen Grand Prix. Her talent peaked when she claimed the European Champion title in 1981, cementing her place in equestrian history.
activity_question: What major title did Liz Edgar win in 1981?
bonus_fact: Showjumping involves guiding horses over a series of obstacles in a specific order and time.

title: A Chef's Fresh Revolution Begins
story: Alice Waters, a culinary pioneer, took her first breath in Chatham Borough, New Jersey in 1944. Born on April 28, she would grow up to change how we think about food. Waters founded the famous restaurant Chez Panisse and championed the farm-to-table movement, bringing fresh, local ingredients to kitchens across America.
activity_question: What famous restaurant did Alice Waters found?
follow_up_question: What movement did Alice Waters champion in the food world?
